data.title: Tap Global to List on London's Junior AIM
data.description:
By Anthony O. Goriainoff

Tap Global said it plans to list on London's junior AIM later in the month.

The regulated cryptocurrency app said Friday that it wasn't raising any new capital nor would it be providing a secondary offering as part of the admission. The company said it had 743.4 million ordinary shares in issue at present.

The board said that AIM was a more appropriate market for the company, allowing it to attract a wider set of investors and improve liquidity over time.

The company said it expects its admission will become effective on June 27, and that its shares will stop trading on the Aquis Stock Exchange on that date.

The company said that Spark Advisory Partners is its nominated adviser and that Tennyson Securities will continue to act as the company's broker after admission.

"The board believes AIM is the right platform for a company of our scale and ambition, providing the profile and access to a wider investor base that will support our growth," Chief Executive Arsen Torosian said.

Write to Anthony O. Goriainoff at anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com

data.title: Alligator Bioscience receives FDA endorsement of mitazalimab Phase 3 dose for pancreatic cancer
data.description:
Lund, Sweden – 13 June 2025 – Alligator Bioscience (Nasdaq Stockholm: ATORX), a clinical-stage biotechnology company developing tumor-directed immunotherapies, today announced that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has provided feedback supporting the selection of the 900 µg/kg dose of its CD40 agonist mitazalimab for the planned Phase 3 study in metastatic pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (mPDAC).

This positive regulatory feedback confirms Alligator’s dose selection and represents a key milestone in the late-stage development of mitazalimab.

“We are very pleased with the FDA’s timely and constructive response. This marks an important step forward as we finalize our Phase 3 program for mitazalimab in one of the most aggressive and underserved cancers. We are now in active partnering dialogues aiming to secure the right partner to take mitazalimab into Phase 3,” said Søren Bregenholt, CEO of Alligator Bioscience.

data.title: China Jushi's Unit to Invest 192 Million Yuan in Limestone Mine Project
data.description:
China Jushi's unit, Jushi Group Jiujiang Lime Industry, plans to invest 191.7 million yuan in a limestone mine project, according to a Shanghai bourse disclosure on Thursday.

The lime manufacturer intends to participate in the auction of the mine's exploration rights and to construct the facilities.

Construction is expected to take two years to complete.

About 1 million tons of limestone will be produced in the mine every year, the Chinese fiberglass maker said.

Notes:

1.

Rule 8 of the Takeover Code (the "Code") sets out the circumstances in which Dealing Disclosures and/or Opening Position Disclosures are required to be made by the parties to an offer, persons acting in concert with them, persons with an interest in relevant securities of 1% or more and exempt principal traders ("EPTs") connected with the parties to an offer.

2.

Where an offeror is marked "Disclosure of dealings and positions in this offeror is not required", dealings and positions in relevant securities of that offeror are not required to be disclosed under Rule 8. This is either because it has been announced that the offer or possible offer is, or is likely to be, solely in cash or because disclosure is not required for some other reason.

3.

Dealings and positions required to be disclosed under Rule 8 of the Code include dealings and positions in derivatives referenced to, and options in respect of, relevant securities of an offeree company and, where appropriate, an offeror (see Note 2 above).

4.

The information set out in the Disclosure Table relating to the relevant securities in issue of an offeree company or of an offeror (and any amendments thereto) has been extracted from the relevant announcement made by such company under Rule 2.9 of the Code. Similarly, details of any deadline set under Rule 2.6 by which a potential offeror must announce either a firm intention to make a bid or that it does not intend to make an offer (or, in the case of Rule 2.6(a), by which the Panel must consent to an extension of the deadline) has been extracted from the relevant announcement made by the offeree company.

5.

Additions and deletions of offeree companies and offerors are reflected in the Disclosure Table throughout the day, following the relevant announcement. Other amendments may also be made throughout the day. In any event, details of the classes of relevant securities issued by an offeree or offeror company, the number of such securities in issue ("NSI") and the International Securities Identification Number ("ISIN") of such securities announced under Rule 2.9 of the Code before 5.00 pm each day will be included in the final version of the Disclosure Table by 5.30 pm that day. The final version of the Disclosure Table issued each day will be marked "Final" and persons required to make Dealing Disclosures and Opening Position Disclosures are advised to use that final version. Any changes to the Rule 2.6 deadline of a potential offeror will also be reflected in the "Final" version of the Disclosure Table, although any such changes will not be shown in the "Amendments" section at the top of the Disclosure Table.

6.

Dealing Disclosures by the parties to an offer, persons acting in concert with them and connected EPTs must be made by no later than 12 noon on the business day (as defined in the Code) following the date of the relevant dealing and Dealing Disclosures by persons with an interest in relevant securities of 1% or more must be made by 3.30 pm on the business day following the date of the relevant dealing.

7.

Opening Position Disclosures by the parties to an offer and by connected EPTs must be made by no later than 12 noon on the 10th business day after the commencement of the offer period or the announcement that first identifies an offeror (as the case may be). Where required, Opening Position Disclosures by persons with interests in relevant securities of 1% or more must be made by no later than 3.30 pm on the 10th business day after the commencement of the offer period or the announcement that first identifies an offeror (as the case may be). Details of the time and date at which an offer period commenced and at which an offeror was first identified are set out in the Disclosure Table. In order to calculate the deadline for Opening Position Disclosures, it is therefore necessary to count forward 10 business days from the relevant date.

8.

Opening Position Disclosures and Dealing Disclosures required under Rule 8 of the Code must be made to a Regulatory Information Service ("RIS").

9.

This Note is applicable if it says "See Note 9 below" by the name of the company in the Disclosure Table above.  The Executive has granted the company a dispensation from the requirements under the Code that announcements must be published via a RIS. The company is instead required to publish all announcements on its website. The Executive has also granted a dispensation from Note 3 on Rule 8 of the Code that disclosures made under Rule 8 of the Code must be made to a RIS. Therefore, Opening Position Disclosures and Dealing Disclosures required under Rule 8 of the Code may be made to the company at the address stated in the announcement that commenced the offer period.

10.

The following are approved Regulatory Information Services:

data.title: Ratos' Sentia Debuts on Euronext Oslo Børs
data.description:
Sentia started trading on Euronext Oslo Børs on Friday following the completion of the bookbuilding for its initial public offering.

The construction arm of Swedish private equity company Ratos (RATO-A.ST, RATO-B.ST) started trading under the symbol SNTIA, according to a Friday release.

data.title: China Jushi's Unit to Inject 155 Million Yuan Into Subsidiary's Capital
data.description:
China Jushi's unit, Jushi Group, plans to inject 155 million yuan into the capital of its subsidiary, Jushi Group Jiujiang Lime Industry, according to a Shanghai bourse disclosure on Thursday.

The amount included 55 million yuan to reduce Jiujiang Lime's asset-liability ratio, which stood at 72.94% as of December 31, 2024. It also included 100 million yuan to fund the construction of the limestone for flux project, the Chinese fiberglass maker said.

data.title: Kyocera: to Sell 108.1M Shares of KDDI for Y249.29B
Steel Prices Steady Amid Geopolitical, Trade Uncertainty
data.description:
Steel rebar futures hovered around CNY 2,980 per tonne on Friday, ending the week largely flat as investors weighed mounting geopolitical tensions and economic headwinds.

The market remained cautious after Israel launched a preemptive strike on Iran’s nuclear facilities, prompting retaliatory drone attacks from Tehran.

The sharp escalation heightened global risk aversion, though its immediate impact on steel demand appeared limited.

Meanwhile, uncertainty surrounding US trade policy continued to cloud the outlook as President Donald Trump threatened to impose unilateral tariffs to pressure foreign governments into trade deals.

Meanwhile, the China Iron and Steel Association warned that a deepening price war in the electric vehicle sector is straining steelmakers.

Automakers are reportedly pressuring suppliers to cut prices on steel plates used in vehicle production, further compressing industry margins.

data.title: Indian shipping stocks defy wider weakness as Mideast tensions spur tanker demand hopes
data.description:
** Indian shipping stocks rise, outliers in a weak market

** Great Eastern Shipping Co rises 6%, Shipping Corp of India jumps 10%

** Jump on the day sentiment-driven, on fears Israel-Iran conflict may widen, which could disrupt oil supply and drive up tanker demand and rates - Aishvarya Dadheech, founder and chief executive of Fident Asset Management

** Crude prices have soared on the day, last up more than 9%, dragging local refiners and lifting oil producers

** YTD, GESC up over 7%, SCI up more than 11%

data.title: Talga Group's Sweden Graphite Mine Has 'Monster Potential,' Says Euroz Hartleys
data.description:
Talga Group's Nunasvaara natural graphite mine, which was recently given a permit by the Swedish Government, carries the potential to be a "monster" anode producer, said Euroz Hartleys in a Thursday note.

The company said on Thursday that all appeals against the Nunasvaara South natural graphite mine exploitation concession have been dismissed by the Swedish Government, and all major permits are now in force for the mine.

The company could expand to produce 425,000 tonnes of anode material a year, which would still meet only a small part of future demand, the note added.

The research firm said that the company plans to build an advanced chemical business with an expected 105,000 tonnes per year of anode in 2031.

Euroz Hartleys maintained its speculative buy rating on Talga Group with a price target of AU$1.33.

data.title: Boustead Singapore Explores Sale of Real Estate Assets to Planned REIT; Shares Up 6%
data.description:
Boustead Singapore is considering selling its logistics and industrial real estate assets to a real estate investment trust to be listed on the Singapore Exchange, according to a Singapore bourse filing on Thursday.

The potential sale is part of a strategic review aimed at unlocking shareholder value.

The company said the review remains exploratory, with no assurance that a transaction will materialize.

Shares of the company were up nearly 6% in recent trading.

data.title: Japan's April Industrial Production Falls 1.1%
data.description:
Japan's industrial production fell 1.1% in April from the previous month, government data showed on Friday, as manufacturers trimmed output.

The seasonally adjusted production index stood at 101.3. Inventories dropped 0.8%, while shipments were flat, rising just 0.1%.

Operating ratio rose 1.3%, while production capacity slipped 0.4%.

data.title: Ocado Draws Down $152 Million Letter of Credit Linked to Kroger Partnership
data.description:
Ocado Group said Thursday it has drawn down the $152 million letter of credit issued by the Bank of Nova Scotia, originally established under its 2018 partnership with The Kroger Co.

The capital contribution was tied to the British online supermarket and technology company's investment in Kroger's online business.

The letter of credit had a maturity date of seven years from the signing of the agreement.

data.title: Enity Holding Begins Trading on Nasdaq Stockholm
data.description:
Enity Holding (ENITY.ST) commenced trading on Nasdaq Stockholm on Friday following the completion of a 1.45 billion-kronor initial public offering.

The specialist mortgage company's main shareholder and EQT's EQT VII-indirectly controlled, Butterfly Holdco, sold 25.5 million existing shares priced at 57 kronor apiece. Meanwhile, an overallotment option of up to 3.8 million additional shares was granted to the joint bookrunners, according to a same-day release.

Enity will not receive any primary proceeds from the offering, with settlement scheduled for June 17.

Nordea Bank, Skandinaviska Enskilda Banken, and ABG Sundal Collier acted as joint bookrunners.
